Create a missing values heatmap

qc_missing_hm.Rd

Makes a ComplexHeatmap object showing a heatmap of missing values in the input data.

Usage

qc_missing_hm(
  data,
  groups,
  sample_labels = NULL,
  column_sort = c("cluster", "group"),
  group_var_name = "",
  show_all_proteins = F
)

Arguments

data

A data frame of intensity data, likely normalized. Rows should be proteins and columns should be samples.

groups

A character or factor vector, listing the group(s) the samples belong to.

sample_labels

Optional, a vector of sample labels to use. If not supplied, defaults to using the column names in the data.

column_sort

How should the columns of the heatmap be sorted? Options are: "cluster"- sort by similarity in missing values, "group"- sort samples by the grouping variable.

group_var_name

The name of the variable being used for group sorting.

show_all_proteins

Should all proteins be shown in missing value heatmap, of only those with missing data? Default is F (only those with missing data).

Value

A grid::gTree object of the ComplexHeatmap, which can be plotted with grid::grid.draw.

On this page
